claim_text: CAB can frame SADS as an assertion-portability problem rather than a variant-classification-only problem.
evidence_basis: CAB/PRF portability theory; SADS use-case table; external comparator/rationale support
caveat: does not validate outcome prediction or cause-of-death inference

claim_text: CAB can distinguish postmortem cause-of-death relevance from predictive family-risk relevance.
evidence_basis: postmortem/family-risk context separation in CAB use-case framing
caveat: does not predict individual family-member risk

claim_text: CAB can route genotype-first / phenotype-negative relatives to PRF-needed rather than deterministic disease reuse.
evidence_basis: PRF framing and genotype-first absent phenotype regime
caveat: does not estimate penetrance or diagnose relatives

claim_text: CAB complements disease-specific channelopathy curation by auditing portability after classification.
evidence_basis: ClinGen channelopathy VCEP external comparator support
caveat: does not replace ClinGen/VCEP or have ClinGen validation

claim_text: Brugada/SADS is a high-value portability boundary because pathogenic meaning may depend on phenotype, trigger/provocation, penetrance, and disease model.
evidence_basis: Brugada/SADS rationale and SCN5A/channelopathy disease-model specificity
caveat: does not validate Brugada risk prediction

claim_text: CAB replaces ClinGen/VCEP or expert curation.
CAB is clinically validated for postmortem interpretation.
CAB improves patient outcomes or family outcomes.
